mentions.note: Chili's CEO Kevin Hawk delivering blowout turnaround: Chili's same-store sales +8.6% vs. 6.1% expected; 25-cent earnings beat; two-year comp over 40%; 19 consecutive quarters of growth; menu simplification cuts costs; value leadership with $3-4 cheaper per-person check vs. competitors; quality upgrades (50% more bacon, domestic ribs) driving volume. Stock down 14% over 12 months despite triple since start 2024; now poised to burst higher.

20 consecutive quarters SSS growth; new chicken sandwich 80% bigger than competitor, priced at $10.99 in three-for-me menu; fighting shrinkflation with value and portion expansion; April off to strong start.
